qa_pairs.question: What is the hottest thing in the known universe?
qa_pairs.answer: The Large Hadron Collider at CERN.

qa_pairs.question: After Helen finishes work, everything she sees turns slightly purple for a few minutes, even though no one else around her is affected the same way. What is her occupation?
qa_pairs.answer: She works in a big virtual green-screen studio, such as a weather presenter.

qa_pairs.question: A factory made a range of furniture for a particular company that was 20% smaller than full size. The furniture is not for petite people, and in fact, it's unlikely it will receive much use at all. What was its purpose?
qa_pairs.answer: It was for show homes on new housing estates, to make the rooms look bigger.

qa_pairs.question: How did the face of an unknown young woman from the 1880s become the most kissed woman in history?
qa_pairs.answer: Her death mask was used as the template for CPR dolls, Resusci-Anne.

qa_pairs.question: A person drives to work in their car. After hearing something on the radio, they leave their car at work and drive home in something else at the end of their shift. They park in a school yard some distance from home, and walk the rest of the way. Why?
qa_pairs.answer: They are a snowplow driver who heard a weather report, took the snowplow home, and parked it nearby so they could start plowing early.

qa_pairs.question: In January 1990, two men from Seattle hit on an idea for a new design of eyeglasses. After selling a million pairs, they closed down their company in 2009, after which time their designs would hit a problem. What were they selling?
In 2015, Canadian politician Sheldon Bergson completely changed his name to trick people into voting for him. What unusual name did he choose?
